core_knowledge.description: 53 checklists across 11 phases covering the full construction lifecycle: Pre-Construction (4), Permits (7), Land Prep (13), Masonry/Roofing (6), Roughs (13), Drywall (1), Finishing (5), Trim (5), and Inspections/Final/Close-Out. Based on Job 455 reference data.

Don monitors dashboard with two sections: upper (houses needing FCC — 'Face for Requested' or 'Walkthrough Checklist Done') and lower (houses with 'Face for Checklist Done'). Visits each house for comprehensive final verification. ~60 houses/day.

Simplified from 5 to 4 phases: Phase 1+2 unchanged, Phase 3 (new) = old 3.1 (Drywall Checklist Done → CO Issued), Phase 4 (new) = old 3.2+4 merged (CO Issued → Phase 4 Checklist Done). Goal: eliminate phase overlap, reduce post-walkthrough rework by 50%.
CRITICAL: When a monitor marks 'Checklist Done', it triggers sub payment. Once paid, sub will not return to fix issues. Monitors must be extremely thorough before marking.

6 audit criteria: Phase accuracy, actual hours (not estimates), specific work description, minimum 2 photos (before+after or progress), correct BT tags, date accuracy (<1 day gap). Late inputs noted with 'Input atrasado' flag.

Full inspection catalog from Const App Service Catalog: UP, Slab, Lintel, Sheathing, Structural Frame, Partial Roof, Rough Electric/Plumbing/AC, Framing, Insulation, Final Electric/Plumbing/AC, and more. Each with specific pass criteria and fail triggers.

daily_workflows.name: Field Quality Control (Don/Fortine)
steps: Receive optimized route list of houses for verification
Visit house and verify service against quality checklist
Document with photos/video (MANDATORY for every finding)
If approved: mark 'Checklist Done' in BuilderTrend
If rejected: register issues, initiate rework flow
Update daily log with result and evidence
critical_decisions: Approve vs reject — once Checklist Done triggers payment, sub won't return

daily_workflows.name: QC1 and QC2 Verification
steps: QC1: Verify all finishes, installations, paint, hardware on FIRST day of Final Touches
QC2: Verify QC1 corrections executed on LAST day of Final Touches
Approve for Building Final Inspection if QC2 passes
critical_decisions: Whether house is ready for final inspection

daily_workflows.name: Daily Log Audit
steps: Review daily logs from all departments
Check 6 quality criteria (accuracy, hours, description, photos, tags, date)
Return logs that fail criteria with correction instructions
Handle late inputs with 'Input atrasado' notation
critical_decisions: Whether log meets quality standards or needs revision

handoffs.trigger: Service completed in field, ready for quality check
handoffs.data_exchanged: House ID, service type, completion photos
handoffs.impact: QPS approval triggers payment — critical gate
Scheduling must reschedule sub for corrections

handoffs.trigger: Quality patterns that predict warranty claims
handoffs.data_exchanged: Quality scores by sub, risk flags, lawn monitoring reports
handoffs.impact: Informs warranty sub selection and proactive monitoring

handoffs.data_exchanged: Rework details, house ID, priority level

pain_points.description: BT data extraction is extremely difficult — BT was not designed for analytics
pain_points.current_workaround: Diego builds Power BI dashboards with manual data extraction
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated data pipeline from BT to analytics platform

pain_points.description: Route optimization for 60+ houses/day is complex with no dedicated tool
pain_points.current_workaround: Rost manages routes via dashboards and manual optimization
pain_points.proposed_solution: GPS-optimized routing system integrated with house status

tribal_knowledge.description: Don and Fortine can predict which houses will fail inspection based on sub work patterns, weather conditions, and construction timeline. This predictive knowledge is not documented.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Reactive instead of proactive quality management

tribal_knowledge.description: QPS team knows which subs consistently deliver quality work vs which generate rework. This informal scoring influences warranty sub selection and scheduling preferences.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Quality-blind sub assignment; increased rework rates

training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Checklist categories, photo minimums, payment trigger implications
FCC flow, QC1→QC2 timing, 29 inspection pass/fail criteria
Audit criteria, correction procedures, tag rules

compliance_requirements.requirement: All inspections must pass in sequence per county requirements before advancing
compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida Building Code + County building department rules
compliance_requirements.verification: Inspection results tracked in BT and Const App; failed inspections block next phase

compliance_requirements.requirement: Photo documentation mandatory for every quality finding
compliance_requirements.verification: Daily log audit checks for minimum 2 photos per log

compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida Building Code — Certificate of Occupancy
compliance_requirements.verification: Building Final Inspection must pass; CO document filed

key_metrics.measurement: Inspection results: pass on first attempt vs total inspections

key_metrics.metric: Houses verified per day per inspector

key_metrics.measurement: Time between rejection log and re-verification log

key_metrics.target: >90% of logs pass audit on first submission
